what_happened: Created a GitHub Actions workflow template for the closedloop-electron repo
why: The template lives in .github/workflow-templates/ (not .github/workflows/) so it is not executed by this repo's GitHub Actions runner — it is only a reference artifact for the target repo
fix_applied: Placed file under .github/workflow-templates/electron-ci.yml with a header comment directing consumers to copy it to the target repo's .github/workflows/
pattern_to_remember: Workflow templates destined for external repos go in .github/workflow-templates/, not .github/workflows/. Add a comment at the top clarifying the intended destination and any TODO placeholders for project-specific values.

what_happened: Needed to reference SHA-pinned action versions matching the codebase convention
why: Existing workflows in .github/workflows/ already use SHA-pinned actions with inline version comments (e.g. actions/checkout@<sha> # v4.2.2). The task specified exact SHAs.
fix_applied: Used the exact SHAs specified in the task for checkout, setup-node, and dependency-review-action; matched the comment style # vX.Y.Z from existing workflows
pattern_to_remember: All GitHub Actions in this repo are SHA-pinned. When adding new actions, use the full commit SHA with a # vX.Y.Z comment. Check existing workflows in .github/workflows/ to see already-pinned versions before looking up new ones.
